title: DriftAnswer-Py: A 12-Item Executable Pilot of Accepted Python Stack Overflow Answers Under Documented Library Drift
description: Build a small executable pilot benchmark of 12 accepted Python Stack Overflow answers whose status under documented `pandas` or `scikit-learn` evolution can be reproduced with old/current environments and official evidence packets. The benchmark's primary task is binary `valid` vs `needs_update`, with `partially_stale` vs `obsolete` retained only as secondary exploratory metadata when confidence is high.
motivation: Recent work already studies Stack Overflow post updating, comment-guided maintenance, and answer enhancement, so the remaining publishable gap is not a new task but a narrowly reproducible artifact. Accepted answers are high-trust developer artifacts, and there is still value in a benchmark that combines accepted-answer focus, documented library drift, and dual-version executable adjudication under a realistic small-scale budget.
proposed_approach: Mine accepted answers from Stack Overflow threads tagged `python` plus exactly one target library tag from `pandas` or `scikit-learn`, then retain only items with deterministic local harnesses, official documentation evidence, and plausible old/current version pairs. Release a 12-item pilot with binary labels, minimal reference repairs for `needs_update` items, and a lightweight evaluation using one local code model under true closed-book, thread-aware, and authority-aware prompting.

related_work.title: A Study of Update Request Comments in Stack Overflow Answer Posts
related_work.authors: Mohammad Sadegh Sheikhaei, Yuan Tian, Shaowei Wang
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper studies answer-maintenance signals in comments, but does not build an executable benchmark of accepted answers with old/current validation.

related_work.title: Towards Better Answers: Automated Stack Overflow Post Updating
related_work.authors: Yubo Mai, Zhipeng Gao, Haoye Wang, Tingting Bi, Xing Hu, Xin Xia, Jianling Sun
related_work.how_it_differs: Soup addresses broad Stack Overflow post updating. DriftAnswer-Py is narrower and claims novelty only in the combination of accepted-answer focus, documented library drift, and dual-version executable adjudication.

related_work.title: Human-Aligned Enhancement of Programming Answers with LLMs Guided by User Feedback
related_work.authors: Suborno Deb Bappon, Saikat Mondal, Chanchal K. Roy, Kevin Schneider
related_work.how_it_differs: ReSOlve/AUTOCOMBAT evaluates feedback-guided answer enhancement rather than a small executable pilot centered on accepted answers and documented library drift.

related_work.title: An Empirical Study of Obsolete Answers on Stack Overflow
related_work.authors: Haoxiang Zhang, Shaowei Wang, Tse-Hsun Chen, Ying Zou, Ahmed E. Hassan
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides the core motivation that Stack Overflow answers age, but not a reproducible executable benchmark artifact.

related_work.title: BUMP: A Benchmark of Reproducible Breaking Dependency Updates
related_work.authors: Frank Reyes, Yogya Gamage, Gabriel Skoglund, Benoit Baudry, Martin Monperrus
related_work.how_it_differs: BUMP is the strongest artifact precedent for executable update benchmarks, but its unit is project-level dependency breakage rather than accepted Stack Overflow answers.

related_work.title: LLMs Meet Library Evolution: Evaluating Deprecated API Usage in LLM-based Code Completion
related_work.authors: Chong Wang, Kaifeng Huang, Jian Zhang, Yebo Feng, Lyuye Zhang, Yang Liu, Xin Peng
related_work.how_it_differs: This work studies generated code under API evolution rather than historical accepted answers with documented drift evidence and repairs.

hypothesis: For accepted Python Stack Overflow answers affected by documented library drift, providing official library evidence improves current-version executable repair over a true closed-book baseline and a thread-only baseline. On a 12-item pilot, the strongest supported claim should be directional and artifact-focused, with binary `valid` vs `needs_update` more reliable than a full three-way stale-status taxonomy.
success_criteria: Release a 12-item executable benchmark artifact with complete metadata, thread text, official evidence, old/current requirements, deterministic harnesses, structured run outputs, and reference repairs for `needs_update` items.
Assign a defensible binary `valid` vs `needs_update` label to every released item, treating `partially_stale` vs `obsolete` as secondary only when subtype confidence is high.
Show that authority-aware prompting repairs more `needs_update` items than the true closed-book baseline using one local code model that fits on the stated hardware budget.

Keep the benchmark construction and evaluation workflow credible within the stated eight-hour total budget by separating construction time from model evaluation time and avoiding claims that require large-scale annotation or screening.
